title: When Shortcuts Go Too Far: The AI Cheating Scandal at Brown University
subtitle: An economics professor’s decision to allow take-home exams has sparked a major debate about academic honesty and the role of artificial intelligence in higher education.

summary.en: A Brown University economics professor noticed suspicious results after allowing students to take exams at home. The class, which usually has a small number of students, saw a massive increase in enrollment this semester. Nearly half of the students received a perfect score on a difficult midterm exam. Professor Roberto Serrano believes that students used artificial intelligence to cheat on their assignments. This situation has led to a wider discussion about how technology is changing the way students learn and perform in school.
summary.zh: 布朗大學一名經濟學教授在允許學生居家考試後，發現了可疑的成績結果。該課程通常選修人數較少，但本學期選課人數大幅增加。在一次高難度的期中考中，近半數學生獲得了滿分。羅貝托·塞拉諾教授認為學生利用人工智慧在作業中作弊。此事件引發了關於科技如何改變學生學習與校內表現的廣泛討論。

paragraphs.en: A serious academic scandal is currently unfolding at Brown University, raising difficult questions about how students use technology to complete their work. The situation centers on an economics course, ECON 1170, taught by Professor Roberto Serrano. After a tragic shooting incident on campus in December 2025, Professor Serrano decided to make his spring 2026 exams 'take-home' to help students during a difficult time. However, this change led to unexpected and troubling results that have now become the focus of a national conversation.
paragraphs.zh: 布朗大學（Brown University）目前正爆發一起嚴重的學術醜聞，引發了關於學生如何利用科技完成課業的棘手質疑。這起事件的核心是一門由羅貝托·塞拉諾（Roberto Serrano）教授所開設的經濟學課程「ECON 1170」。在二零二五年十二月校園發生一起悲劇性的槍擊事件後，塞拉諾教授為了在艱難時期協助學生，決定將二零二六年春季學期的考試改為「居家考試」。然而，這項變動卻導致了意想不到且令人不安的結果，如今已成為全國討論的焦點。

paragraphs.en: According to Ars Technica, the course usually attracts a small group of highly dedicated students. In the past, the average grade for the midterm exam typically ranged between 65 and 80 percent. Because the exams were now take-home, Professor Serrano made them even more challenging than usual. Despite the increased difficulty, the results were shocking. The class size grew to 86 students, and the average score on the midterm was 96 out of 100. Most notably, 40 students—nearly half of the class—received a perfect score of 100.
paragraphs.zh: 根據科技媒體「Ars Technica」報導，這門課程通常只吸引少數極為認真的學生。過去，期中考的平均成績通常落在六十五分到八十分之間。由於改為居家考試，塞拉諾教授將考題設計得比以往更具挑戰性。儘管難度提升，結果卻令人震驚。該班級人數增加至八十六人，而期中考平均分數竟高達九十六分（滿分一百分）。最引人注目的是，有四十名學生——幾乎佔了全班的一半——拿到了滿分一百分。

paragraphs.en: Professor Serrano, who has been very open about his concerns, believes these results are not a sign of hard work, but rather evidence of widespread cheating using generative AI. Generative AI is a type of computer software that can create text, solve problems, and write essays in seconds. While these tools can be helpful for brainstorming, they can also be used to write entire exam answers without the student actually learning the material. As reported by Ars Technica, Serrano noted that he cannot accept such results, as they suggest that students are choosing shortcuts instead of engaging with the difficult economic concepts he teaches.
paragraphs.zh: 對於此事直言不諱的塞拉諾教授認為，這些成績並非學生努力的證明，而是大規模利用生成式人工智慧作弊的證據。生成式人工智慧是一種能在幾秒鐘內生成文字、解決問題並撰寫文章的電腦軟體。雖然這些工具對腦力激盪很有幫助，但學生也可能利用它們撰寫整份考卷，而無需真正學習課程內容。正如「Ars Technica」所報導，塞拉諾指出他無法接受這樣的結果，因為這顯示學生選擇走捷徑，而非深入鑽研他所教授的高難度經濟學概念。

paragraphs.en: This incident at Brown University is part of a larger trend in education. A recent survey of students at Princeton University found that nearly 30 percent of students admitted to using AI to cheat on at least one assignment or exam. Experts suggest that while students at top universities are clearly intelligent, they are also under immense pressure to succeed. When students are busy and competitive, AI tools can look like an easy way to save time. However, educators warn that this behavior undermines the purpose of university education. If students rely on machines to do their thinking, they miss the opportunity to develop the critical skills they will need in their future careers.
paragraphs.zh: 布朗大學發生的這起事件，是教育界更大趨勢的一部分。近期一項針對普林斯頓大學（Princeton University）學生的調查發現，近百分之三十的學生坦承曾在至少一項作業或考試中利用人工智慧作弊。專家指出，頂尖大學的學生固然聰明，但也承受著巨大的成功壓力。當學生忙碌且競爭激烈時，人工智慧工具看起來就像是節省時間的捷徑。然而，教育工作者警告，這種行為破壞了大學教育的目的。如果學生依賴機器代勞思考，他們將錯失培養未來職涯所需關鍵技能的機會。

paragraphs.en: Professor Serrano has shared his story with several news outlets, including El País and Inside Higher Ed, to highlight the severity of the issue. He argues that universities must find ways to address this behavior before it becomes the new normal. The scandal has left the university community struggling to find a balance between trusting students and ensuring that grades accurately reflect what a student has actually learned. Some faculty members are now reconsidering the use of take-home exams, while others are looking for new ways to test students that cannot be easily solved by AI.
paragraphs.zh: 塞拉諾教授已向包括西班牙《國家報》（El País）與《高等教育內幕》（Inside Higher Ed）在內的多家新聞媒體分享了他的遭遇，以凸顯此問題的嚴重性。他主張大學必須在這種行為成為新常態之前，設法加以應對。這起醜聞讓大學社群陷入兩難，必須在信任學生與確保成績能準確反映學生實際學習成果之間尋求平衡。部分教職員正重新評估是否繼續採用居家考試，而其他人則在尋找無法輕易被人工智慧破解的新型評量方式。

paragraphs.en: As the investigation into the exam results continues, the case serves as a warning for other schools. The ease with which students can now access powerful AI tools means that traditional methods of testing may no longer be enough to prevent cheating. For students, the challenge is to resist the temptation of the 'easy path.' For universities, the challenge is to create environments where learning is valued more than a high grade. Whether this scandal will lead to stricter rules or a new approach to teaching remains to be seen, but it is clear that the relationship between technology and academic honesty has changed forever.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著對考試結果的調查持續進行，此案對其他學校而言是一項警訊。學生如今能輕易取得強大的人工智慧工具，意味著傳統的評量方式可能已不足以防止作弊。對學生而言，挑戰在於抵擋「走捷徑」的誘惑；對大學而言，挑戰則在於創造一個重視學習勝過高分的環境。這起醜聞是否會導致更嚴格的規定，或是促成新的教學方法仍有待觀察，但顯而易見的是，科技與學術誠信之間的關係已永遠改變了。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To have something as the main subject or focus.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於名詞用法（中心），這裡作為動詞片語使用。文中：The situation centers on an economics course, ECON 1170, taught by Professor Roberto Serrano.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To become involved with or show a deep interest in a subject or idea.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此處並非指「訂婚」或「交戰」，而是指學生對學術概念的深入理解與思考。文中：Serrano noted that he cannot accept such results, as they suggest that students are choosing shortcuts instead of engaging with the difficult economic concepts he teaches.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To gradually weaken or damage something, especially someone's authority or a process.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此詞在學術或正式文章中常見，容易被誤認為與「挖礦」有關，但實際意指破壞基礎。文中：However, educators warn that this behavior undermines the purpose of university education.

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: "We cannot choose to become idiots": The AI cheating scandal roiling Brown University

previousArticles.title: AI Cheating Scandal at Brown University Raises Questions About Future of Learning
previousArticles.summaryEn: A professor at Brown University noticed unusually high grades in his economics class after allowing take-home exams. The course, which usually has few students, saw a large increase in enrollment this semester. Roberto Serrano, the professor, believes many students used artificial intelligence to complete their work. This situation highlights the growing challenge of academic honesty in the age of AI. Many students face high pressure to succeed, which may lead them to use technology as a shortcut.

quiz.question: What change did Professor Serrano make to the ECON 1170 course in spring 2026, and what was the outcome regarding student performance?

options.A: He reduced the course difficulty, which led to a higher number of students enrolling in the class.
options.B: He implemented take-home exams, which resulted in an unusually high average score of 96.
options.C: He allowed students to use AI tools, which caused the average grade to drop compared to previous years.
options.D: He increased the number of exams, leading to 40 students dropping out due to the workload.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，Serrano 教授為了體恤學生，將考試改為「居家考試」（take-home exams），且刻意增加了難度，結果平均分數卻高達 96 分，且有 40 人獲得滿分。選項 A 錯誤，因為教授反而增加了難度；選項 C 錯誤，因為文章並未提到教授允許使用 AI，且分數是上升而非下降；選項 D 錯誤，文章提到的是班級人數成長至 86 人，而非退選。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why might students at top universities be particularly susceptible to using AI for assignments?

options.A: They lack the basic academic foundation to understand the complex economic concepts taught.
options.B: They are encouraged by their professors to use AI to improve their critical thinking skills.
options.C: They face significant pressure to perform well, making time-saving shortcuts appear attractive.
options.D: They believe that traditional testing methods are outdated and no longer relevant to their future careers.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第四段，專家指出頂尖大學的學生雖然聰明，但承受極大的成功壓力（immense pressure to succeed）。當學生忙碌且競爭激烈時，AI 工具成為節省時間的捷徑。選項 A 過度推論，文章並未說學生缺乏基礎；選項 B 與文章內容相反，教授們反而擔心 AI 會破壞教育目的；選項 D 雖然提到考試方法可能過時，但這不是學生使用 AI 的主要動機。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the author mean by the phrase 'the easy path'?

options.A: A method of learning that focuses on practical, hands-on experience.
options.B: The act of using technology to bypass the effort required for genuine academic work.
options.C: A new teaching strategy that helps students complete their assignments more quickly.
options.D: The traditional way of studying that relies on textbooks rather than digital tools.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文中提到學生面臨挑戰是「抗拒『簡單路徑』（easy path）的誘惑」，結合前後文討論 AI 協助作弊的脈絡，這裡的「簡單路徑」指的就是利用 AI 快速完成作業而不進行深度思考。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 C 將其視為正面教學策略是錯誤的；選項 D 與原文強調的「科技帶來的捷徑」不符。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the impact of AI on higher education?

options.A: AI tools have proven that traditional university education is no longer necessary for success.
options.B: Professors at Brown University are solely responsible for the rise in academic dishonesty.
options.C: The convenience of AI is forcing universities to rethink how they assess student learning and academic integrity.
options.D: Students are increasingly choosing to cheat because they no longer respect their professors' authority.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。整篇文章透過 Brown 大學的案例，探討了 AI 如何改變學術誠信，並指出大學必須在信任學生與確保評量真實性之間找到平衡，且傳統考試方法已不足以應對。選項 A 過於極端，文章並未否定大學價值；選項 B 將責任歸咎於教授有失公允；選項 D 將作弊動機簡化為對教授的不尊重，忽略了壓力與科技便利性的核心問題。
